name: cibuildwheel (Python Wheel Builder for CI)

auth.notes: CI environment; PyPI upload via twine or trusted publishing

performance.notes: CI build tool; build time depends on package complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Builds Python wheels for multiple platforms in CI. GitHub Actions: uses: pypa/cibuildwheel@v2. pyproject.toml [tool.cibuildwheel] config. Supports Linux (manylinux), macOS (universal2), Windows. Python 3.8-3.13. Build targets: CIBW_BUILD='cp3{9,10,11,12}-*'. Cross-compilation for Apple Silicon. QEMU for Linux ARM. Test each wheel before upload. Integrates with GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Azure Pipelines. The standard tool for distributing C extension packages on PyPI.

gotchas: Complex for non-C extension packages (use flit/hatch instead)
Docker required for Linux manylinux builds locally
QEMU for ARM builds is slow in CI
macOS universal2 requires careful dependency handling
best_when: Distributing C/Cython/Rust extension packages on PyPI
Building wheels for multiple Python versions and platforms
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for scientific Python packages
Cross-platform binary distribution of Python extensions
avoid_when: Pure Python packages (flit or hatch much simpler)
Internal packages not distributed on PyPI
summary: cibuildwheel builds Python wheels across Linux/macOS/Windows for multiple Python versions in CI. GitHub Actions: uses pypa/cibuildwheel@v2. Handles manylinux, universal2, cross-compilation. The standard tool for distributing C/Cython/Rust extension packages on PyPI. Config in pyproject.toml [tool.cibuildwheel]. Not needed for pure-Python packages — use flit or hatch instead.
